summary: Postinstall dropper fetches and executes remote Windows binary from anonymous Cloudflare tunnel
details: warp-dependency@1.0.1 declares `postinstall: node index.js` in package.json, so `npm install` automatically runs the shipped index.js. index.js is heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io-style string-array rotation with identifiers like _0x5f33cf/_0x32fa) to hide its behavior: it downloads a binary named `bss.exe` from https://beneath-state-sports-colorado.trycloudflare.com/page — an anonymous, ephemeral Cloudflare tunnel unrelated to any legitimate publisher — writes it to the package directory, and immediately executes it via child_process.exec. There is no version pin, no hash verification, and no signature check on the fetched executable. The package's declared description ("Mac UI for Windows Toolkit") is inconsistent with its actual behavior and the name appears crafted to masquerade as a dependency of the Warp Terminal ecosystem. Installing this package results in attacker-controlled code execution on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
